symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden-onset rapid palpitations during exertion with near-syncope, self-resolving with rest
symptoms.character: heart pounding fast and hard, associated with dark/woozy vision

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exercise-induced palpitations with near-syncope is a classic presenting feature of ARVC, reflecting exercise-triggered re-entrant ventricular arrhythmia in the fibrofatty RV substrate.
Exertional presyncope can occur with vasovagal syncope, but palpitations as the presenting sensation (rather than lightheadedness/greying out) are less typical.
Sudden-onset, self-terminating rapid palpitations is a compatible pattern for paroxysmal SVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive worsening of exertional palpitations over the preceding two months
symptoms.onset: gradual onset approximately two months prior, worsening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A progressive pattern of exercise-induced arrhythmia symptoms over weeks-to-months is consistent with an advancing structural arrhythmic substrate rather than a single benign episode, and is a recognised pattern preceding sudden cardiac events in ARVC.

Simple vasovagal syncope does not typically show a progressive escalating pattern of exertional palpitations building over months; it is usually situational and episodic without a worsening trajectory.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of vasovagal prodrome — palpitations preceded the dizziness/near-syncope with no preceding warmth, nausea, or tunnel vision

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Typical vasovagal syncope has a recognisable autonomic prodrome (warmth, nausea, tunnel vision, pallor) preceding loss of consciousness. Its absence, with palpitations as the primary preceding sensation, argues strongly against a simple reflex mechanism.

An arrhythmic prodrome (sudden palpitations immediately before near-syncope, without gradual autonomic build-up) is a recognised discriminating feature favouring a primary arrhythmic cause of the event.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of sudden unexplained death in a maternal uncle at age 30

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden unexplained death in a first/second-degree relative under approximately 40-45 years is a major diagnostic criterion for inherited arrhythmic cardiomyopathies including ARVC, and independently mandates urgent cardiology referral regardless of the patient's own symptom severity.

A significant family history of sudden cardiac death is not a feature of simple reflex/vasovagal syncope and should prompt active exclusion of an inherited arrhythmic cause before accepting a benign diagnosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Continued unrestricted high-intensity training despite escalating symptoms, with no prior cardiac screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Continued strenuous exercise without prior cardiac screening in the presence of an unrecognised arrhythmic substrate increases cumulative risk of a malignant ventricular arrhythmia; this does not change the diagnostic probability itself but is highly relevant to immediate safety planning.

symptoms.symptom_name: Frequent premature ventricular contractions, palpable on cardiovascular examination (clinician-obtained finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frequent PVCs, particularly with a left bundle branch block morphology arising from the RV, are a recognised finding in ARVC reflecting the arrhythmogenic substrate. Requires clinician examination/ECG to detect — not obtainable via telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right ventricular heave and mildly elevated jugular venous pressure on examination (clinician-obtained finding)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early right heart structural involvement can produce a palpable RV heave and mildly elevated JVP, though these are non-specific and require clinician examination to elicit and interpret in context.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epsilon waves on 12-lead ECG (investigation result, pending test in source)

investigation_details.expected_result: Possible epsilon waves, T-wave inversion in V1-V3 beyond age 14, and/or frequent ventricular ectopy — cannot be confirmed without the actual tracing; presumed pending test in the source note.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without a 12-lead ECG, key diagnostic markers for ARVC cannot be identified, and the arrhythmic substrate remains unquantified. The AI Doctor cannot order this directly via telehealth and must refer for urgent in-person assessment where it can be obtained.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Epsilon waves and anterior T-wave inversion are relatively specific (though insensitive) ECG markers included in the ARVC Task Force diagnostic criteria.

symptoms.symptom_name: RV fibrofatty replacement on cardiac MRI (investigation result, pending test in source)

investigation_details.expected_result: Presumed pending test in the source note — would assess for RV wall motion abnormality, dilatation, and fibrofatty myocardial replacement, the definitive structural criterion for ARVC.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without cardiac MRI, definitive structural diagnosis of ARVC cannot be made; this investigation is arranged by cardiology after initial ECG/Holter screening, not ordered directly via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: RV fibrofatty replacement with associated wall motion abnormality on cardiac MRI is the definitive major structural criterion for ARVC diagnosis per Task Force Criteria.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of (1) sudden exertional palpitations with near-syncope, (2) a progressive two-month symptom trajectory, (3) absence of a vasovagal prodrome, and (4) family history of sudden unexplained death at age 30 constitutes a high-risk inherited arrhythmia constellation. No single feature is individually diagnostic, but together they mandate urgent cardiology referral and immediate activity restriction regardless of the eventual confirmed diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line investigation for any exertional palpitations/syncope with concerning family history. Looks for epsilon waves, T-wave inversion, and ectopy patterns suggestive of ARVC or other inherited arrhythmia. Cannot be performed via telehealth — requires urgent in-person or ED assessment.
Quantifies ventricular ectopy burden and may capture a further arrhythmic episode; standard part of the ARVC/inherited arrhythmia work-up arranged by cardiology.

Definitive structural imaging to assess for RV fibrofatty replacement and wall motion abnormality; arranged by cardiology after initial screening, not a same-day telehealth action but should be flagged as part of the expected pathway.
First-line, more widely available structural assessment often performed alongside or before cardiac MRI to assess RV size/function and exclude other structural causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Genetic testing for desmosomal gene mutations
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not a first-line telehealth or acute-phase recommendation — genetic testing is arranged by a specialist cardiac genetics service after clinical/imaging diagnosis is established, and is not appropriate for the AI Doctor to recommend as an immediate next step.
